title: 10 best herbal essential oils for shingles
intro_desc: Herbal essential oils for shingles are natural remedies derived from plants that help alleviate the symptoms and accelerate the healing process of shingles, a viral infection caused by the reactivation of the varicella-zoster virus. The best essential oils for shingles include Melaleuca alternifolia, Zingiber officinale, Lavandula angustifolia, Eucalyptus globulus, Echinacea purpurea, Aloe barbadensis, Capsicum annuum, Calendula officinalis, Hypericum perforatum, and Aloe vera. If left untreated, shingles can cause severe pain, itching, and discomfort, leading to sleep deprivation, mood changes, and decreased productivity, ultimately impacting daily life and relationships.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Tea tree essential oils helps with shingles because of its potent antiviral properties, which can help combat the varicella-zoster virus that causes shingles. The oil's anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties also reduce swelling, redness, and itching associated with shingles. Additionally, tea tree oil's decongestant properties can ease congestion and promote healing by improving blood circulation to the affected area. As a natural remedy, it can provide relief from the discomfort and pain caused by shingles, helping to speed up the recovery process.
remedy_properties: Antimicrobial properties: Tea tree essential oil has been shown to have antibacterial, antiviral, and antifungal properties, which may help combat the herpes simplex virus that causes shingles.
Inflammation reduction: The oil's anti-inflammatory compounds may help reduce pain, itching, and inflammation associated with shingles lesions.
Wound healing: Tea tree essential oil has been traditionally used to promote wound healing, which may aid in the treatment of shingle blisters and crusts.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Rich in cineole, a compound that has anti-inflammatory and antiviral properties, making it effective against the virus that causes shingles.
Buds: Contain melaleuca alternifolia, which has been shown to reduce pain and inflammation associated with shingles.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 10-15 drops of high-quality melaleuca alternifolia essential oil.
Add the essential oil to a carrier oil such as coconut or olive oil in a ratio of 3% to 5%.
Mix well and apply topically to affected area using gentle massage strokes.
Apply the mixture three times daily for at least 7 days.
Consult with a healthcare professional before using essential oils on sensitive areas or children.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: English lavender essential oils helps with shingles because of its natural anti-inflammatory and antiviral properties. Lavender oil has been shown to soothe irritated skin, reduce pain and discomfort associated with shingles, and even help to reduce the duration of the outbreak. Its calming effects can also promote relaxation and reduce stress, which is often exacerbated by the discomfort caused by shingles. Additionally, lavender's antiviral properties may help to combat the varicella-zoster virus that causes shingles, reducing the severity of symptoms and promoting faster recovery.
remedy_properties: Antiviral and antiseptic properties: Lavender essential oil has been shown to possess antimicrobial properties that can help combat the varicella-zoster virus (VZV) responsible for causing shingles, reducing the risk of infection and promoting healing.

Anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties: The analgesic and anti-inflammatory compounds in lavender essential oil may help alleviate the painful and itchy symptoms associated with shingles, providing relief from discomfort and inflammation.

Calming and relaxing properties: Lavender's calming effects can help reduce stress and anxiety, which are common accompaniments to shingles. This relaxation response can also promote a sense of well-being, helping to mitigate the emotional distress often experienced during shingles outbreaks.
remedy_parts: Roots: English lavender roots contain a high concentration of flavonoids, which have been shown to have antiviral properties that can help combat shingles.
Leaves: Lavender leaves have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce the pain and discomfort associated with shingles.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality lavandula angustifolia flowers in mid-summer when they are in full bloom and dry them thoroughly within 24 hours.
Combine one cup of dried lavandula angustifolia flowers with a carrier oil such as coconut or olive oil at a ratio of 10:1 by weight in a clean glass jar.
Place the mixture in a dark glass container and steep it for 14 days, shaking the jar daily to ensure the essential oils are extracted evenly.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into another clean glass container, discarding the solids and reserving the infused oil.
Transfer the infused oil to small dark glass bottles, label them, and store them in the refrigerator for up to 6 months before using the essential oil for shingles treatment.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ceylon cinnamon essential oils helps with shingles because of its potent antiviral and anti-inflammatory properties. The oil's active compounds, such as cinnamaldehyde, have been shown to inhibit the growth of viruses that cause shingles, including herpes zoster virus. Additionally, Ceylon cinnamon essential oil has a warming and soothing effect when applied topically, which can help alleviate the painful and itchy symptoms associated with shingles, providing relief from discomfort and promoting healing.
remedy_properties: Antiviral properties: Ceylon cinnamon essential oil's antiviral properties can help combat the herpes virus that causes shingles, reducing its spread and severity.
Anti-inflammatory properties: The anti-inflammatory compounds in Ceylon cinnamon essential oil can help alleviate symptoms such as pain, itching, and swelling associated with shingles outbreaks.

Antioxidant properties: Ceylon cinnamon essential oil's antioxidant properties can help protect the skin from damage caused by free radicals, reducing the risk of post-herpetic neuralgia (PHN) and promoting faster healing.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: The rhizome of Cinnamomum verum contains cinnamic acid and other bioactive compounds that have been shown to possess anti-inflammatory, antiviral, and antimicrobial properties, making it effective in alleviating the symptoms of shingles.
Barks: The bark of Ceylon cinnamon is rich in quercetin, a flavonoid with potent antioxidant and anti-inflammatory activities, which can help reduce pain and inflammation associated with shingles.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality cinnamomum verum bark from sustainable sources ensuring freshness.
Dry the harvested bark using shade drying or machine drying methods for 7-10 days.
Steam distill the dried bark in a clean and well-maintained apparatus at 100°c for 4 hours.
Filter the distilled oil through a coffee filter paper or a microfilter to remove impurities.
Store the extracted essential oil in dark glass bottles with tight-fitting lids at room temperature for up to 2 years.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Curry plant essential oils helps with shingles because it contains antiviral and anti-inflammatory properties that can help alleviate the symptoms of this painful condition. The oil's active compounds, such as terpenes and sesquiterpenes, have been shown to inhibit the growth of the herpes zoster virus, which causes shingles. Additionally, the oil's analgesic and antispasmodic properties can help reduce nerve pain and itching associated with shingles, providing relief from discomfort and promoting a faster recovery.
remedy_properties: Antiviral properties: Curry plant essential oil has been shown to exhibit antiviral activity against certain viruses, including herpes simplex virus 2 (HSV-2), which is responsible for causing shingles.
Anti-inflammatory properties: The oil's anti-inflammatory compounds can help reduce redness, swelling, and pain associated with shingle lesions, providing relief from discomfort.

Antioxidant properties: Curry plant essential oil contains antioxidants that can help protect the skin from further damage caused by free radicals, promoting healing and reducing the risk of scarring.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the curry plant contain sesquiterpenes, which have anti-inflammatory properties that help alleviate the pain and discomfort associated with shingles.
Barks: The barks of the curry plant contain flavonoids, which have antiviral properties that help combat the varicella-zoster virus that causes shingles.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried helichrysum italicum leaves and flowers in the early morning.
Dry the plant material thoroughly for at least two hours using a clean cloth or paper towels.
Steam distill 50 grams of dried helichrysum italicum material using 500 ml of distilled water for 30 minutes.
Separate the essential oil from the distillation mixture using a separating funnel and store in dark glass bottles.
Blend 10 drops of the helichrysum italicum essential oil with 1 tablespoon of carrier oil such as coconut or olive oil.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Pot marigold essential oils helps with shingles because of its anti-inflammatory and antiviral properties. The oil contains a compound called lutein, which has been shown to reduce inflammation and promote healing in the skin. When applied topically, pot marigold essential oil can help alleviate the discomfort and pain associated with shingles rash, while also reducing the duration and severity of the outbreak. Additionally, its antiviral properties may help shorten the length of the viral replication phase, allowing the body to recover more quickly.
remedy_properties: Antiviral property: Pot marigold essential oil has been shown to have antiviral activity against various viruses, including herpes simplex virus (HSV), which is the underlying cause of shingles.

Anti-inflammatory property: The essential oil contains compounds like lutein and zeaxanthin that can help reduce inflammation and pain associated with shingles, making it an effective natural remedy for soothing skin irritation.

Wound-healing property: Pot marigold essential oil has been used topically to promote wound healing due to its antimicrobial properties, which can help prevent infection and speed up the recovery process in shingles cases.
remedy_parts: Roots: Potent anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties in the roots make them an effective component in essential oils to soothe and heal shingle lesions.

Flowers: The bright yellow flowers of the pot marigold contain flavonoids, which have been shown to reduce inflammation and promote wound healing, making them a valuable addition to shingle treatment essential oils.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried calendula flowers at dawn when they are still dewy for optimal potency.
Dry the harvested calendula flowers in a warm, dark place for 1-2 weeks to reduce moisture content.
Steam distill the dried calendula flowers using a dedicated essential oil distillation apparatus with 6 cups of water for 4 hours and collect the resulting oil.
Filter the collected essential oil through cheesecloth or a coffee filter to remove impurities and sediment.
Store the filtered calendula officinalis essential oil in dark glass bottles with tight-fitting lids at room temperature for up to 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Tasmanian blue gum essential oils helps with shingles because of its potent anti-inflammatory and antiviral properties. The oil's active compounds, such as cineole and terpinen-4-ol, have been shown to reduce pain and inflammation associated with shingles, while also inhibiting the replication of the herpes zoster virus that causes the condition. By applying topically or inhaling the oil, individuals can experience relief from symptoms like itching, burning, and numbness, promoting a faster recovery from shingles.
remedy_properties: Antiviral properties: Tasmanian Blue Gum essential oil contains compounds like eucalyptol and globulol, which have been shown to inhibit the growth of viruses, including herpes simplex virus (HSV), the virus that causes Shingles.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The presence of terpenoids in Tasmanian Blue Gum essential oil makes it a potent anti-inflammatory agent, which can help reduce pain, swelling, and inflammation associated with Shingles.

Antimicrobial properties: The essential oil's antimicrobial properties can help combat secondary bacterial infections that often occur as a result of Shingles, reducing the risk of complications and promoting faster healing.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in bioactive compounds that have anti-inflammatory and antiviral properties, making them effective against shingle-related pain and inflammation.
Barks: Contains a high concentration of phenolic acids, which have antimicrobial and antioxidant properties that can help soothe and reduce the severity of shingles symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest eucalyptus globulus leaves by hand or using mechanical harvesters in the early morning dew when aromatic compounds are highest.
Dry harvested leaves in a warm well-ventilated area for 7-10 days to reduce moisture content and preserve potency.
Steam distill dried eucalyptus globulus leaves at 100°c with 1:5 leaf-to-water ratio using a steam distillation apparatus for 4 hours.
Filter and collect distilled oil through a paper coffee filter or glass fiber filter pad into dark glass bottles with tight-fitting lids.
Store final essential oil in a cool dark place away from direct sunlight and heat sources at room temperature for up to 12 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Wild geranium essential oils helps with shingles because of its antiviral properties, which can help reduce the severity and duration of the outbreak. The oil's antimicrobial properties also work to combat fungal and bacterial infections that may be contributing to the development of shingles. Additionally, wild geranium essential oil has anti-inflammatory properties, which can help alleviate the painful skin rash and itching associated with shingles. Overall, using wild geranium essential oil may provide natural relief from the discomfort and embarrassment caused by shingles.
remedy_properties: Antiviral properties: Wild Geranium essential oil has been shown to inhibit the growth and replication of certain viruses, including herpes simplex virus, which is the cause of shingles.
Anti-inflammatory properties: The oil's anti-inflammatory compounds may help reduce pain, itching, and inflammation associated with shingles rash and blisters.
Antioxidant properties: Wild Geranium essential oil contains antioxidants that can help protect the skin from damage caused by oxidative stress, which is often exacerbated during a shingles outbreak.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in sesquiterpenes and other bioactive compounds, making them effective in reducing inflammation and pain associated with shingles.
Rhyzomes: Containing a high concentration of geraniol, which has anti-inflammatory and antiviral properties, helping to alleviate symptoms of shingles such as itching and burning.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh geranium maculatum leaves and flowers at dawn for maximum oil content.
Dry the harvested material in a warm, dark place for seven days to reduce moisture.
Steam distill the dried geranium maculatum material using a clevenger apparatus for three hours.
Collect the resulting essential oil and store it in a dark glass bottle with a tight lid at room temperature.
Allow the essential oil to mature for two weeks before use, shaking the bottle daily to ensure proper blending.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Nutmeg essential oils helps with shingles because of its antiviral and anti-inflammatory properties. The oil contains compounds like caryophyllene and limonene, which have been shown to inhibit the replication of herpes virus, reducing the severity and duration of shingles outbreaks. Additionally, nutmeg's warming and soothing effects can help relieve painful rashes and numbness associated with shingles, providing a sense of comfort and relief.
remedy_properties: Antiviral properties: Nutmeg essential oil has been shown to possess antiviral activity, which can help combat the varicella-zoster virus that causes shingles.
Analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties: The oil's analgesic and anti-inflammatory compounds may help alleviate the pain, itching, and inflammation associated with shingles lesions.
Antioxidant properties: Nutmeg essential oil's antioxidant activity can help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the body, which can contribute to the healing process of shingles.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antimicrobial compounds, making them effective against viral infections like shingles.
Rhyzomes: Contain sesquiterpenes, which have anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, helping to soothe shingle-related pain and inflammation.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh myristica fragrans leaves and seeds from the plant at dawn for maximum potency.
Dry the harvested materials in small batches using a food dehydrator on low heat for 24 hours.
Blend 2 parts dried myristica fragrans leaves and seeds with 1 part carrier oil such as coconut or olive oil in a glass jar.
Store the mixture in a cool dark place for 14 days to allow for infusion, shaking the jar daily to ensure even extraction.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth into another glass container and discard the solids, then store the essential oil in a dark glass bottle at room temperature.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Chamomile essential oils helps with shingles because it possesses anti-inflammatory, antispasmodic, and antiseptic properties that can provide relief from the excruciating pain, itching, and discomfort associated with this condition. The oil's ability to soothe irritated skin and reduce inflammation can help alleviate symptoms of shingles, such as blisters and rashes. Additionally, chamomile's calming effects can promote relaxation, reducing stress and anxiety often triggered by shingles outbreaks, allowing individuals to feel more comfortable and at ease during the recovery process.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Chamomile essential oil reduces inflammation, which helps to alleviate the painful symptoms of shingles, such as burning, tingling, and numbness.
Antiviral: The antiviral properties of chamomile essential oil can help to combat the varicella-zoster virus that causes shingles, reducing the severity and duration of outbreaks.
Calming and soothing: Chamomile's calming effects can help to reduce anxiety, stress, and itching associated with shingles, promoting relaxation and comfort.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of chamomile are valued for their ability to soothe and calm inflammation, making them effective in alleviating the pain and discomfort associated with shingles.
Flowers: The flowers of chamomile contain apigenin, an antioxidant that has anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce swelling and promote healing in shingles lesions.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 10 fresh matricaria chamomilla flowers at dawn on a dry day for maximum potency and quality.
Dry the harvested flowers in a warm, dry place for 7-10 days to reduce moisture content.
Steam distill the dried flowers using 300ml of water and 30g of flowers for 1 hour and 15 minutes.
Filter the distilled oil through a coffee filter or cheesecloth to remove impurities and sediment.
Store the final essential oil in a dark glass bottle with a tight-fitting lid, protected from light and heat, for up to 2 years.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Thyme essential oils helps with shingles because of its potent antiviral properties, which can aid in reducing the severity and duration of the outbreak. The oil's ability to soothe inflammation and pain can also provide relief from the discomfort associated with shingles. Additionally, thyme essential oil contains compounds that have antimicrobial effects, helping to prevent secondary infections and promote wound healing. By incorporating thyme essential oils into a topical treatment or diffusing it in the air, individuals may experience faster recovery times and reduced symptoms of shingles.
remedy_properties: Antiviral Properties: Thyme essential oil has antiviral properties that can help combat the varicella-zoster virus, which causes shingles. This property helps to reduce the severity and duration of the outbreak.

Antifungal Properties: Thyme's antifungal properties can help to prevent secondary fungal infections that often occur in people with compromised immune systems, such as those with shingles. This property can also aid in wound healing.

Inflammation-Reducing Properties: Thyme essential oil has anti-inflammatory compounds that can help to reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms such as pain, itching, and discomfort associated with shingles.
remedy_parts: Roots: Thyme roots have antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties, making them effective in soothing and healing shingles sores.
Leaves: Thyme leaves contain compounds like carvacrol and thymol, which have antiviral and antimicrobial effects, helping to reduce the severity of shingle symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 2 cups of fresh thymus vulgaris leaves in the morning after dew has dried.
Cleanse the harvested leaves by gently rinsing them with distilled water and air-drying for 2 hours.
Steam-distill the cleaned leaves using a steam distillation apparatus with 50 grams of plant material per 100 milliliters of distilled water at 100°c for 4 hours.
Filter the resulting essential oil through a coffee filter or cheesecloth to remove impurities and separate the oil from the hydrosol.
Bottle and store the final thymus vulgaris essential oil in a dark glass bottle with a tight-fitting lid at room temperature for up to 6 months.

supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat shingles with herbal essential oils is by blending Tea Tree oil, known for its antiviral properties, with Lavender oil, which soothes and calms the skin. Apply this blend topically to the affected area using a carrier oil like Coconut or Olive oil. Additionally, inhaling the steam of Eucalyptus oil can help relieve symptoms such as itching and discomfort. This natural approach can aid in reducing inflammation and promoting healing.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to shingles that are treated with herbal essential oils are herpes simplex, genital warts, and varicella. Tea tree oil has been shown to have antiviral properties, making it effective against these conditions. Other essential oils like lemongrass, lavender, and oregano also exhibit antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help soothe and calm affected areas. Additionally, essential oils like peppermint and eucalyptus can provide relief from pain and itching associated with these ailments.

remedies.remedy_desc: Melaleuca alternifolia essential oils helps with shingles because it has potent antiviral and anti-inflammatory properties. The oil's active compound, corymbia, has been shown to inhibit the replication of the varicella-zoster virus, which causes shingles. Additionally, the oil's anti-inflammatory properties help reduce pain and inflammation associated with shingles, promoting healing and skin regeneration. By incorporating Melaleuca alternifolia essential oils into a treatment plan, individuals may experience relief from shingles symptoms, including pain, itching, and skin lesions.
remedy_constituents: Cineole: Acts as an antiviral and anti-inflammatory agent, helping to reduce the severity and duration of shingles symptoms.
Limonene: Exhibits antiviral and antiseptic properties, which may help combat the herpes zoster virus that causes shingles and reduce the risk of complications.
Caryophyllene oxide: Possesses anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may help alleviate pain, itching, and other symptoms associated with shingles.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of Melaleuca alternifolia are commonly used to produce essential oils for shingles due to their high content of cineole, a compound with anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties.

Buds: The buds of Melaleuca alternifolia are also used to produce essential oils for shingles, as they contain a high concentration of cineole, which helps to reduce pain and inflammation associated with the condition.

remedies.remedy_desc: Zingiber officinale essential oils helps with shingles because of its anti-inflammatory and antiviral properties. The oil has been traditionally used to reduce pain and discomfort associated with shingles, often caused by the reactivation of the varicella-zoster virus. The essential oil's analgesic and antiseptic properties help to soothe and calm the affected skin, reducing the severity of shingles symptoms such as itching, burning, and blistering. Additionally, its immune-boosting effects may help the body's natural defenses fight off the virus.
remedy_constituents: Gingerols: These sesquiterpenes have anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce pain and inflammation associated with shingles.
Shogaols: These sesquiterpenes have analgesic and anti-inflammatory effects that can help alleviate the discomfort and pain caused by shingles.
Curcumenol: This sesquiterpene has potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that can help reduce the severity of shingles symptoms, such as pain, itching, and inflammation.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: Rhyzomes are used due to their high content of gingerols and shogaols, which have anti-inflammatory and antiviral properties that help alleviate shingles symptoms.
Roots: Roots are used for their ability to reduce pain and inflammation associated with shingles, thanks to their rich content of gingerols and other bioactive compounds.
Buds: Buds are used due to their high concentration of bioactive compounds that have anti-inflammatory and antiviral properties, helping to manage shingles symptoms and promote healing.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 30-40 fresh rhizomes of zingiber officinale by carefully digging them up from their natural habitat.
Steam distill the rhizomes in a distillation apparatus at 100-110°c for 4-6 hours to release the oil.
Collect the distillate and separate the essential oil from the hydrosol using a separator funnel.
Filter the essential oil through a paper filter to remove any remaining impurities and improve its clarity.
Store the final product in dark glass bottles with tight-fitting lids to preserve its potency and freshness.

remedies.remedy_desc: Lavandula angustifolia essential oils helps with shingles because of its anti-inflammatory and antiviral properties. It has been traditionally used to soothe and calm the skin, reducing pain and discomfort associated with shingles. The oil's ability to promote relaxation and reduce stress also helps alleviate the emotional distress caused by the condition. Furthermore, its antimicrobial properties can help combat secondary infections and promote wound healing, making it a potential natural remedy for shingles relief. Its application may also help alleviate itching and numbness.
remedy_constituents: Linalool: Helps with shingles by reducing inflammation and pain due to its analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties.
Linalyl acetate: Contributes to relief from shingles by exhibiting antiviral activity, which helps in controlling the replication of the varicella-zoster virus.
Caryophyllene: Shows potential in alleviating shingles symptoms by exhibiting anti-inflammatory and analgesic effects, thus reducing pain and discomfort associated with the condition.
remedy_parts: Flowers: Lavender flowers are the most commonly used part for making essential oils due to their high concentration of lavender oil, which has anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties that can help soothe and calm shingles symptoms.

Leaves: Lavender leaves contain a smaller amount of essential oil than flowers, but still have therapeutic properties, such as antiseptic and anti-inflammatory effects, that can help alleviate shingles symptoms.

Stems: Lavender stems contain a significant amount of essential oil, particularly linalool and linalyl acetate, which have anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties that can help reduce shingles pain and inflammation.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 liter of fresh lavandula angustifolia flowers when the plant is in full bloom and the flowers are in good condition.
Steam distill the harvested flowers at 100°c for 3 hours using a distillation apparatus with a 1:3 ratio.
Collect the distillate and separate the essential oil from the water by decantation within 30 minutes.
Store the essential oil in a clean glass bottle with a tight-fitting lid in a cool dark place.
Label the bottle with the essential oil's name date of distillation and concentration within 24 hours.

remedies.remedy_desc: Eucalyptus globulus essential oils helps with shingles because it has potent anti-inflammatory and antiviral properties. The oil's active compounds, such as eucalyptol, help to reduce pain and inflammation associated with shingles. Its decongestant properties also help to ease the discomfort and itching caused by the rash. Additionally, eucalyptus oil has antimicrobial properties that help to prevent the spread of the virus and promote a faster recovery. This makes it a popular natural remedy for shingles sufferers.
remedy_constituents: Eucalyptol (1,8-Cineole): This terpene has anti-inflammatory and antiviral properties, which can help reduce the severity of shingles symptoms, including pain and inflammation.
α-Pinene: This terpene has antiviral and anti-inflammatory effects, which can help combat the varicella-zoster virus that causes shingles and reduce the risk of complications.

Caryophyllene: This sesquiterpene has anti-inflammatory and immunomodulatory properties, which can help reduce pain and inflammation associated with shingles, as well as modulate the immune response to the virus.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves are the most commonly used part for making eucalyptus essential oil due to their high oil content and fragrance.
Barks: The barks are used for making eucalyptus essential oil, which is particularly useful in treating shingles due to its antiviral properties.
Stems: The stems are also used for making eucalyptus essential oil, which is valued for its anti-inflammatory properties that can help soothe shingles symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 cup of eucalyptus globulus leaves from mature trees at dawn when the dew is still present.
Steam distill the eucalyptus globulus leaves at 3 bar pressure for 2 hours to release essential oil.
Collect the essential oil from the distillation apparatus and separate it from the water through decantation.
Store the collected essential oil in a dark glass bottle with a tight-fitting lid at room temperature.
Use the essential oil within 12 months of production and check it regularly for signs of spoilage.

remedies.remedy_desc: Echinacea purpurea essential oils helps with shingles because it possesses potent anti-inflammatory and antiviral properties. The oil's active compounds, such as alkylamides and caffeic acid, have been shown to stimulate the immune system and reduce the severity of shingles symptoms. Additionally, Echinacea purpurea's antimicrobial properties help combat viral infections that cause shingles, while its anti-inflammatory properties ease the associated pain and discomfort. This makes it a promising natural remedy for shingles relief and recovery.
remedy_constituents: Cichoric acid: Helps with shingles by reducing inflammation and inhibiting the spread of the herpes zoster virus.
Icariin: Contributes to shingles relief by its anti-inflammatory and antiviral properties, which aid in reducing pain and discomfort.
Caffeic acid: Exhibits antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which help to alleviate shingles symptoms by reducing the severity of pain and inflammation.
remedy_parts: Roots: Echinacea roots contain compounds with anti-inflammatory properties that help alleviate shingles symptoms.
Leaves: Echinacea leaves are rich in antioxidants and flavonoids that help reduce pain and inflammation associated with shingles.
Flowers: Echinacea flowers have been used to make essential oils that have antiviral and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help in treating shingles.
remedy_recipe: Harvest echinacea purpurea flowers in their full bloom stage when they have a rich deep purple color and a strong fragrance.
Dry the harvested flowers in a warm place with good air circulation for at least 2 weeks to reduce moisture content.
Steam distill the dried flowers using a steam distillation apparatus at a temperature not exceeding 100 degrees celsius for 2 hours.
Separate the essential oil from the hydrosol using a separatory funnel and filter the oil through a paper filter to remove impurities.
Store the extracted echinacea purpurea essential oil in a dark glass bottle sealed with a tight lid and refrigerate at 4 degrees celsius.

remedies.remedy_desc: Aloe barbadensis essential oils helps with shingles because of its anti-inflammatory and antiviral properties. The oil contains compounds like aloin and aloe-emodin, which have been shown to reduce pain and inflammation associated with shingles. Additionally, the oil's antimicrobial properties help combat the varicella-zoster virus, the cause of shingles. By promoting skin healing and reducing discomfort, Aloe barbadensis essential oil can alleviate shingles symptoms, such as itching, burning, and redness, and support the body's natural recovery process.
remedy_constituents: Aloe-emodin: Acts as an anti-inflammatory and antiviral agent, helping to reduce the severity and duration of shingles symptoms.
Lupeol: Exhibits anti-inflammatory and antiviral properties, which may help alleviate pain and itching associated with shingles.
Furanocoumarins (e.g. aloin and aloe-derivative coumarins): Show antiviral activity, inhibiting the replication of the varicella-zoster virus, the causative agent of shingles.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves are used due to their high concentration of aloin, a compound that has anti-inflammatory properties helpful in soothing shingles symptoms.
Rhyzomes: The rhyzomes are used due to their rich content of vitamins, minerals, and fatty acids, which help in reducing inflammation and promoting skin healing.
Barks: The barks are used due to their antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which aid in reducing pain and inflammation associated with shingles.
remedy_recipe: Extract 100-200 grams of fresh aloe barbadensis gel from 2-3 mature plants using a sharp knife or a spoon.
Steam distill the aloe gel using a still apparatus at 100-120 degrees celsius for 2-3 hours.
Collect 20-50 milliliters of the distilled aloe oil and transfer it to a clean glass container immediately.
Filter the aloe oil through a layer of activated charcoal to remove impurities and improve clarity for 30 minutes.
Store the filtered aloe oil in a cool dark place in an airtight glass container for up to 6 months.

remedies.remedy_desc: Capsicum annuum essential oils helps with shingles because of its anti-inflammatory and antiviral properties. The capsaicin present in Capsicum annuum has been shown to reduce the severity and duration of shingles symptoms. It helps to ease the pain and discomfort associated with shingles, and also reduces the formation of new blisters. By reducing inflammation and promoting healing, Capsicum annuum essential oils can help alleviate shingles symptoms and support the body's natural healing process. It can also aid in reducing stress and promoting relaxation.
remedy_constituents: Capsaicin: Capsaicin has analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce pain and inflammation associated with shingles.
Eugenol: Eugenol has antiviral properties that may help combat the herpes zoster virus that causes shingles, reducing its severity and duration.
Caryophyllene: Caryophyllene has anti-inflammatory and immunomodulatory properties that may help alleviate pain, inflammation, and itching associated with shingles.
remedy_parts: Fruits: The fruits of Capsicum annuum are commonly used to make essential oils because they are the most abundant and accessible part of the plant.
Leaves: The leaves of Capsicum annuum are used to make essential oils due to their high concentration of bioactive compounds, which may provide therapeutic benefits.
Seeds: The seeds of Capsicum annuum are used to make essential oils because they contain a high amount of oleoresin, a complex mixture of bioactive compounds.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 250g of fresh capsicum annuum fruits when they are fully ripe and have reached their peak color intensity.
Dry the fruits in a single layer at 35c for 3 days to remove excess moisture and promote oil production.
Steam distill the dried capsicum annuum fruits for 3 hours to extract the essential oil from the plant material.
Collect and separate the essential oil from the distillation apparatus using a separatory funnel and a clean glass container.
Filter the extracted essential oil through a 5 micron filter paper to remove any impurities and improve its quality.

remedies.remedy_desc: Calendula officinalis essential oils helps with shingles because of its anti-inflammatory and antiviral properties. It has been traditionally used to soothe and calm irritated skin, reducing the discomfort and pain associated with shingles. The oil's natural ability to reduce inflammation can help alleviate the burning sensation and itching caused by the rash. Additionally, Calendula's antimicrobial properties can help prevent secondary infections, promoting a faster recovery from shingles. Its topical application can provide relief and support the healing process.
remedy_constituents: Isorhapontigenin: Acts as an anti-inflammatory agent, reducing pain and inflammation associated with shingles.
Quercetin: Exhibits antiviral properties, which can help combat the herpes zoster virus responsible for shingles.
α-Humulene: Possesses anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, promoting wound healing and reducing the risk of secondary infections associated with shingles.
remedy_parts: Flowers: The flowers of Calendula officinalis are commonly used to make essential oils due to their high content of anti-inflammatory and antiviral compounds.
Leaves: The leaves of Calendula officinalis are also used to make essential oils, as they contain sesquiterpenes and other compounds that help to soothe and calm the skin.
Roots: The roots of Calendula officinalis are sometimes used to make essential oils, as they contain triterpenoid saponins and other compounds that have anti-inflammatory and antiviral properties.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 500g of fresh calendula officinalis flowers when in full bloom at dawn on a clear day.
Dry the flowers in a single layer on paper towels for 2-3 days in a well-ventilated area.
Steam distill the dried flowers in a distillation apparatus at 100-110°c for 3-4 hours.
Collect the distilled essential oil and separate it from the water using a separatory funnel.
Store the calendula officinalis essential oil in a dark glass bottle with a tight-fitting lid at room temperature.

remedies.remedy_desc: Hypericum perforatum essential oils helps with shingles because of its anti-inflammatory and antiviral properties, which work to reduce pain and discomfort associated with the condition. The oil's ability to soothe and calm the skin can also help alleviate the intense itching and burning sensations often experienced by shingles patients. By reducing inflammation and promoting healing, Hypericum perforatum essential oils may provide relief and support the body's natural recovery process.
remedy_constituents: Naphthodianthrones (e.g., Hyperforin, Hypericin): These constituents have potent antiviral and antiphlogistic properties, which help to reduce inflammation and viral replication associated with shingles.
Flavonoids (e.g., Quercetin, Kaempferol): These flavonoids exhibit anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help to alleviate the pain, itching, and discomfort associated with shingles.

Phenolic acids (e.g., Gallic acid, Ferulic acid): These phenolic acids have antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, which help to prevent secondary infections and reduce the severity of shingles symptoms.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Leaves are used due to their antiviral and anti-inflammatory properties that help soothe shingles symptoms.
Flowers: Flowers are used because they contain flavonoids and naphthoquinones that have antiviral and anti-inflammatory effects.
Stems: Stems are used due to their high concentration of hyperforin, a compound that has been shown to reduce shingles symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh hypericum perforatum flowers at peak bloom in the morning to ensure optimal oil quality.
Dry the flowers using a food dehydrator at 95°f for 2 hours or air dry them for 1 week.
Steam distill the dried flowers using a distillation apparatus with a 10:1 water to flower ratio for 3 hours.
Separate the essential oil from the hydrosol using a centrifuge or decantation and filter the oil using a coffee filter.
Store the essential oil in a dark glass bottle with a tight-fitting lid and label it clearly for future use.

remedies.remedy_desc: Aloe vera essential oils helps with shingles because of its anti-inflammatory and antiviral properties. The oil's key compounds, such as aloe-emodin, help reduce pain and discomfort associated with shingles. Its antiviral properties target the herpes zoster virus that causes shingles, potentially reducing the severity and duration of the outbreak. Aloe vera essential oil's soothing and calming effects also help alleviate the itchy and burning sensations often experienced during shingles, promoting faster recovery and relief.
remedy_constituents: Furanocoumarins: These compounds have been found to possess antiviral properties, which can help in reducing the replication of the varicella-zoster virus that causes shingles.
Aloin: This anthraquinone glycoside has anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, which can help in relieving the pain, itching, and inflammation associated with shingles.

Phenolic compounds (including Caffeic acid and Ferulic acid): These compounds have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help in reducing the oxidative stress and inflammation caused by the varicella-zoster virus, thereby promoting healing and reducing symptoms of shingles.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The gel from the leaves is often used to create a soothing and calming essential oil that helps alleviate shingles symptoms.
Buds: Aloe vera bud essential oil is used for its antiviral and anti-inflammatory properties, which can aid in reducing shingles pain and discomfort.
Seeds: Aloe vera seed essential oil is used for its antiseptic and antifungal properties, which can help in preventing infection and promoting wound healing in shingles cases.
remedy_recipe: Gather 1000ml aloe vera gel from mature leaves in a sterile environment taking about 30 minutes.
Steep the aloe vera gel in a double boiler with a water bath for 30 minutes to 1 hour.
Strain the infused aloe vera gel through a cheesecloth or a fine mesh sieve to remove particles taking about 15 minutes.
Use a distillation apparatus to separate the essential oil from the water taking about 2 hours.
Collect the extracted essential oil in a dark glass bottle and store it in a cool place for up to 6 months.
